original:
The link settles on feed of volatiles futures scrolling across the crest of the Tri-Tachyon corporation for just a moment before the comm-link connects you with a perfectly coiffed junior secretary.

"The Tri-Tachyon Corporation welcomes you to Eochu Bres. Their smile is uncannily bright. May we presume you wish to accept Director Glamor-Rotanev's invitation?"

"Fantastic. A priority flight path has been pre-approved for your convenience. The Tri-Tachyon Corporation is pleased to wish you a most profitable meeting with the Director of Special Acquisitions."

The uncanny smile re-appears, perfectly, once more. It's a little unsettling.

option_line: ZGR_visitOptDirect:Take a shuttle to visit Director of Special Acquisitions Glamor-Rotanev
original: Take a shuttle to visit Director of Special Acquisitions Glamor-Rotanev

"Fantastic." The face maintains an uncanny smile. "The Director is most eager to meet you."

An apologetic - but not guilty - expression forms. "Unfortunately, the automated defenses of the Tri-Tachyon Corporation have flagged your ID as a hostile and your shuttle would be gunned down upon approach. Please contact customer service to resolve this misunderstanding."

"We hope you have a pleasant and profitable day."

The smile re-appears.

option_line: 2:ZGR_visitOptDirect:Take a shuttle to visit Director of Special Acquisitions Glamor-Rotanev
original: ZGR_visitOptDirect:Take a shuttle to visit Director of Special Acquisitions Glamor-Rotanev

Your shuttle descends into the young atmosphere of Eochu Bres, through towering battlements of clouds. A spray of rain darkens your viewport for a moment before it is wiped clean by the heat and speed of your descent.

The flight path immediately deviates from the general approach corridor which had started ticking off minor use-fees from the Tri-Tachyon owned flight control: general volume access fee, priority flight lane fee, service fee, customer satisfaction insurance charge, advertisement bypass fee.

The notifications self-delete, their fees automatically refunded, as the towering central arcologies of Xanadu emerge from the mist.

Jagged mountain peaks ring the horizon. Gleaming monoliths glitter, spears of capital upthrust through the glowering flesh of the world. Skyarco II grows before you, prismatic.

Your shuttle alights on a landing pad held at some distance from the facade by an airy support rib. It looks impossibly thin, but on touch down feels as solid as a coherent M-type asteroid.
